file_insights..auto-claude/specs/002-robust-authentica: Updated task statuses from 'pending' to 'completed' for subtasks 2.3 and 2.4, reflecting successful implementation of authentication token refresh features.
Comprehensive verification guide created with detailed test cases, error type matrix, and acceptance criteria status tracking for token refresh notifications.
file_insights.lib/app/app.dart: Added TokenRefreshNotificationListener wrapper around MaterialApp to ensure notifications are visible from any screen in the app.
file_insights.lib/app/di/modules/auth_module.dart: Added missing import for resend_verification_email use case to support authentication flow.
file_insights.lib/features/auth/presentation/notifiers: New notifier implementation that converts TokenRefreshService events into user-facing notifications with different types (silent, warning, error) and appropriate button options.
Auto-generated code for Riverpod annotations and providers.
file_insights.lib/features/auth/presentation/providers: New provider setup for TokenRefreshService integration with dependency injection.
Auto-generated code for Riverpod provider annotations.
file_insights.lib/features/auth/presentation/widgets/t: New widget implementation that displays notifications via SnackBars and Dialogs based on error type with appropriate user actions.

patterns_discovered.notification_pattern: Two-tier notification system with silent success (no UI) and visible failures (SnackBar/Dialog) based on error severity
patterns_discovered.error_handling_matrix: Structured error classification system with different UI elements and user actions per error type: network errors show SnackBar with auto-retry, credential errors show Dialog with re-auth option, max retries shows Dialog with both retry and sign-in options
patterns_discovered.widget_integration_pattern: Notification listener wrapped at app level (app.dart) to ensure global visibility and consistent user experience across all screens
patterns_discovered.state_management_pattern: Riverpod-based state management using providers and notifiers for clean separation between service events and UI presentation

gotchas_discovered.user_preferences_limitation: User notification preferences not implemented due to lack of existing preferences system (marked as future enhancement)
gotchas_discovered.multiple_notification_prevention: Need to prevent multiple overlapping notifications during consecutive refresh failures (mentioned in verification guide)
gotchas_discovered.manual_testing_difficulties: Some test cases require backend manipulation (like expired refresh tokens) making manual verification challenging
gotchas_discovered.dependency_timing: Import added to auth_module.dart suggests potential missing dependency issue during development

approach_outcome.implementation_approach: Followed established Flutter/Flutter_Riverpod patterns with clear separation of concerns between service layer (TokenRefreshService), notification handler (TokenRefreshNotificationHandler), and UI presentation (TokenRefreshNotificationListener)
approach_outcome.quality_metrics: High - comprehensive verification document created with test cases, error matrix, and acceptance criteria tracking; proper auto-generated code files maintained
approach_outcome.completeness: Successfully implemented all core requirements: silent refresh on success, user-friendly error messages, and appropriate user actions based on error type; partial on user preferences (future enhancement)
approach_outcome.technical_execution: Clean architecture with proper Riverpod integration, well-structured notification types, and appropriate UI elements per error scenario

recommendations: Implement user notification preferences system to allow users to control refresh notification verbosity
Add notification history feature for debugging and user awareness of refresh events
Implement advanced retry controls with user-configurable settings (retry count, backoff strategy)
Add unit tests for TokenRefreshNotificationHandler to ensure proper error classification and notification generation
Consider adding vibration or sound options for critical refresh failure notifications
Implement proper cleanup/dispose mechanisms for notification listeners to prevent memory leaks
